learning_outcomes.description: Critically examine the significance of consultation, referral and a multidisciplinary approach to the care of women and neonates with critical illness in pregnancy labour and the puerperium, including rural and remote settings.
Analyse the aetiology, management and apply skills including monitoring, assessment and care of the woman and baby for a range of emergency situations in pregnancy, labour, birth and the puerperium.

Using contemporary and appropriate evidence, critically review the pathophysiology of, diagnosis, and evidence for treatment of, and expected outcomes associated with critical illness and life-threatening emergencies of significance to midwifery practice.

Analyse and critically review the contemporary and relevant evidence used to inform midwifery care of women with a critical illness or life-threatening emergency, with a particular focus on the use of digital health technology to improve health outcomes.

Advocate culturally responsive strategies to support women and their families as they are challenged by and deal with the physical and psychological complications of a critical illness or a life-threatening emergency in pregnancy, labour, birth and the puerperium.

assessments.due_date:
13/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

Please submit your Oral Presentation via the link provided in the assessment folder of the Blackboard course on the day of your scheduled presentation.

Please note this assessment will be recorded via Zoom for marking and moderation purposes.
assessments.task_description:
In the first Inquiry Based Learning [IBL] tutorial of the course, you will nominate one of the next five IBL tutorials in which to give a fifteen-minute oral presentation. The presentation time is 15-minutes with 5 minutes for questions and answers. The presentation will be timed by the assessor. An Oral presentation in the IBL tutorial provides you with an opportunity to practice your communication skills. Presenting findings to your group as a real-life audience can help strengthen these skills. The target audience for this presentation are colleagues in the practice setting, for example midwives, doctors, and allied health professionals.

Your topic will be generated as an identified learning outcome or directly as a critical issue from the prior IBL tutorial. Presentations from the previous IBL tutorial will be presented at the start of the next IBL tutorial.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This piece of assessment is to be submitted in the course Blackboard via the provided assessment link on the day of your scheduled presentation. By submitting your presentation, you are certifying that it is your original work and that where you have used the ideas or writing of other authors this has been acknowledged according to accepted academic guidelines.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
28/08/2026 - 28/08/2026

The quiz will open on Friday 28th August 2026 8am and close on Friday 28th August 2330. Once commenced the quiz will be available for a maximum of 60 minutes.
assessments.task_description: This is an individual assessment and must be completed on your own device according to the UQ’s Academic Integrity Pledge. The 60-minute quiz will include a mix of both multiple choice, and short answer questions based on the content from weeks 1-4 of the course  Maternal and Newborn Critical Illness
assessments.submission_guidelines: This will be an off campus, online non-invigilated assessment on the platform Blackboard Ultra. If you do not have access to your own device please see UQ borrowing service
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: You will receive a mark of 0 if this assessment is submitted late.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This assessment will examine the learning objectives of the MIDW2105 course content. Questions will be derived from lecture content, readings, and provided course content weeks 5-13. The exam will be, online invigilated on campus in this course using the Inspera Assessment Platform. Inspera Assessment provides additional functionality to complement the existing suite of digital learning tools at UQ with a greater range of question types and accessibility provisions. Further details, including an Inspera demo test to familiarise yourself with the functionality, and where to go for support can be found in the course companion Blackboard site.

Criteria & Marking: UQ Students: Please access the profile from Learn.UQ or mySI-net to access the marking criteria for this assessment.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

Lecture Series

In this course clinical experts in their respective fields ( Specialist Consultant Obstetricians, Clinical Specialist Midwives) will present the face to face lecture series, lectures will be recorded for review and study purposes however, attendance is highly recommended.
